data.title: Here's What Top Analysts Expect from Broadcom Stock (AVGO) Ahead of Q2 Earnings on June 3
data.description:
The ongoing artificial intelligence (AI) boom has boosted investor sentiment toward chip stocks, driven by massive demand for semiconductors and related infrastructure to support complex AI workloads. All eyes are now on Broadcom's (AVGO) fiscal second-quarter earnings on June 3. AVGO stock has gained 29% year-to-date, fueled by AI-led demand for the company's custom chips and networking products. Several top analysts are bullish on Broadcom stock ahead of Q2 FY26 earnings. Let's look at the reasons for their optimism.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Broadcom to report Q2 FY26 EPS (earnings per share) of $2.40, reflecting 52% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise more than 47% to $22.12 billion.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Did Oracle Stock (ORCL) Jump 11% Today, 5/29/26?
data.description:
Shares in Oracle (ORCL), the enterprise software giant shifting towards becoming an AI cloud company, climbed by 10.84% on Friday, adding to its roughly 7% gain the previous day. The rally coincides with JPMorgan (JPM) kicking off coverage of the stock with a Buy rating and a $210 price target, which implies about 3% upside from the ORCL closing price of $203.70 on Thursday.

data.title: Truist Lifts Alphabet (GOOGL) Stock Price Target; What's Driving the Optimism?
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) stock has risen about 22% year-to-date and nearly 122% over the past year. Artificial intelligence (AI)-led demand for Google Cloud, rising adoption of TPU (tensor processing unit) chips, and integration of AI features across the company's offerings have boosted investor sentiment. On Friday, Truist analyst Youssef Squali raised his price target for GOOGL stock to $430 from $415 and reaffirmed a Buy rating, as he believes that the market is underestimating Google Cloud's revenue.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

High conviction AMZN bears now have this Tradr ETF

Squali also raised his price target for Amazon (AMZN) stock to $320 from $310, saying that the Street's consensus estimates underestimate the company's revenue and capital expenditure. Overall, Squali expects both Alphabet and Amazon to grow "faster for longer" on higher spending.

Truist Analyst Is Bullish on GOOGL Stock

Squali raised his estimates for Google Cloud revenue for Fiscal 2026 and Fiscal 2027 to $102.5 billion and $152.2 billion, respectively, from his prior projections of $90.6 billion and $130 billion.

The 4-star analyst attributed his upgraded Google Cloud top-line estimates to the potential contribution from the recently announced $200 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic and the related revenue recognition from the backlog. Notably, Squali expects TPU consumption to start contributing to Google Cloud revenue in late Fiscal 2026, with expansion expected in Fiscal 2027 and beyond.

The analyst's base case scenario for the Google-Anthropic deal assumes an incremental $20 billion contribution to revenues in Fiscal 2026 and $30 billion in Fiscal 2027, before becoming more consistent at $50 billion in Fiscal 2028 to 2030. Additionally, Squali listed other near-term catalysts for Google Cloud, including growing contributions from other new and existing deals, including partnerships with Meta Platforms (META) and Apple (AAPL), encouraging demand trends for Google Enterprise Agent Platform, and the sale of TPU chips to enterprise clients.

It is worth noting that Squali expects a corresponding rise in Google's capex, which he believes is not fully reflected in the Street's current expectations. The analyst currently expects capex of $189 billion in Fiscal 2026 and $250 billion in Fiscal 2027, compared with consensus estimates of $187 billion and $246 billion.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy Now?

Like Squali, most analysts are bullish on GOOGL's prospects. Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 27 Buys and five Holds. The average GOOGL stock price target of $427.50 indicates 12.4% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) Stocks Jump on Big Price Target Boost from Susquehanna
data.description:
Shares of Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) rallied on Friday and hit all-time highs after a Top-rated Susquehanna analyst, Mehdi Hosseini, issued a bullish call on the memory sector. He lifted price targets on both stocks, citing faster-than-expected gains in chip pricing.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

SNDK has a long & short ETF? Explore SNXX & SNDQ

For SanDisk, he raised his price target to $3,250 (91.7% upside potential) from $2,000 and kept a Buy rating. Hosseini said its latest checks show Q2 DRAM prices rising 50%-60% from last quarter, slightly better than earlier expectations of 50%. NAND pricing is also strong, climbing 75% to 100% quarter-over-quarter. He said this data gives him more confidence that memory makers can keep higher margins through 2026.

The analyst issued a similar upgrade for Micron stock, raising the price target to $1,750 (80.23% upside potential) from $600 while maintaining a Positive rating. Hosseini said the same trends, strong DRAM price gains and steady NAND prices, are lifting earnings expectations across the whole memory group. The firm is now raising estimates for every memory maker it covers.

Overall, he said the steady rise in average selling prices, along with better supply control and tight HBM supply, points to stronger margins than the market expected.

Is MU a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, MU stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 27 Buys and three Holds assigned in the last three months. At $804.26, the average Micron stock price target implies a 17.17% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Moves Closer to Robotaxi Launch after Texas Driverless Approval
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) has received authorization in Texas to operate driverless vehicles commercially, a key step for its planned robotaxi service. The approval follows Senate Bill 2807, which took effect on May 28 after being passed by the 89th Texas Legislature. The law creates a statewide framework for companies operating automated vehicles on public roads, including driverless passenger transport and freight services. In practical terms, it gives Tesla a clearer legal path to launch commercial robotaxi operations in Texas.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

Under the new rules, Tesla and other autonomous-vehicle companies can self-certify that their vehicles and technology are ready for driverless use. They must still follow Texas traffic laws, maintain proper registration, title, and insurance, use compliant automated driving systems, and record onboard activity while handling system failures safely. This structure may speed up the approval process because companies can rely on their own internal readiness checks rather than waiting through a more drawn-out regulatory review.

At the same time, Tesla CEO Elon Musk shared a video showing Cybercab units driving autonomously off the property at Gigafactory Texas. That matters because Cybercab production reportedly started at Giga Texas in April, and the vehicles are now being shown moving without drivers on factory grounds. When taken together, the Texas authorization and Cybercab progress suggest that Tesla is moving closer to a commercial driverless ride-hailing launch.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $403.86 per share implies 7.2% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Jump to New Highs as Trump Nears Iran Deal Verdict; Oil Extends Decline
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) rallied to new highs on Friday, rounding out a strong month for stocks.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Trump held a meeting in the Situation Room with top officials to provide a "final determination" on a deal with Iran. He mentioned the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the removal of mines in the waterway, and the destruction of Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium as key conditions for the U.S. to accept the deal.
  • The U.S. would lift its blockade on Iranian ports if the conditions are satisfied and begin negotiating nuclear restrictions for Iran. "No money will be exchanged, until further notice," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "Other items, of far less importance, have been agreed to."
  • Brent Crude oil (BZ) fell by over 1% and secured its largest monthly loss since 2020. Furthermore, U.S. gasoline prices declined 3.5 cents overnight, marking the largest one-day fall in 17 years.
  • Dell (DELL) skyrocketed higher after reporting that its AI server revenue surged 757% to $16.1 billion during the first quarter. The company's overall revenue accelerated by nearly 88%.
  • Software stocks continued to rise, highlighted by strong returns from ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), Datadog (DDOG), Palantir (PLTR), and CrowdStrike (CRWD).
  • Truist analyst Youssef Squali raised his Amazon (AMZN) price target to $320 from $310 and his Alphabet (GOOGL) target to $430 from $415.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Palantir Stock (PLTR) Just Had Its Best Day in a Year. Here's Why
data.description:
The stock of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) closed 9% higher on May 29, its best one-day performance in a year.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The rally was sparked by a rebound in software stocks. Specifically, Palantir's stock got a boost from rally in the stock of Snowflake (SNOW) following that company's blowout earnings report earlier in the week. Snowflake's earnings sparked a sector-wide rally that has lasted for two consecutive trading sessions.

Palantir's stock has gained 13% over the last week, its best performance since April 2025, according to market data. Palantir, Snowflake and other software companies have seen their share prices take a beating this year over worries that artificial intelligence (AI) will disrupt their business models.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: S&P 500 Top Stock Gainers Today, 5/29/26
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) advanced to a new record high on Friday, closing out May with a 4.8% gain.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Today's Top-Performing Sector 

Information technology was the top-performing sector on Friday. Dell Technologies (DELL) led the charge after reporting a blowout first-quarter earnings report, highlighted by AI server revenue growth of 757% to $16.1 billion. In addition, software stocks continued to rally higher, with NetApp (NTAP), IBM (IBM), and ServiceNow (NOW) posting strong returns.

Several information technology stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Dell Technologies (DELL)
  • NetApp (NTAP)
  • IBM (IBM)
  • ServiceNow (NOW)
  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the consumer staples sector faced the largest drawdown as investors rotated out of the defensive sector and into more speculative areas of the market. Clorox (CLX) led the group to the downside after CEO Linda Rendle announced she would step down due to health reasons and start a search for the next CEO. Costco (COST) also closed lower after reporting its fiscal third-quarter earnings.

Notable consumer staples stocks trading lower include:

  • Clorox (CLX)
  • Costco (COST)
  • Altria Group (MO)
  • Kimberly-Clark Corp (KMB)
  • Walmart (WMT)
data.author: Eddie Pan
